management_credibility.rationale: The 8-K filed August 28 — the last day of the evidence window — disclosed a non-cash pre-tax pension settlement charge of approximately $25 million expected in Q1 fiscal 2024, representing an unforecast one-time earnings headwind that would likely have surprised investors when digested heading into September 5.

cited_evidence.quote: Net interest expense for the quarter was $4.7 million compared to $0.6 million last year, driven by higher interest rates and borrowings.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The sharp rise in net interest expense from $0.6M to $4.7M year-over-year is a direct macro impact (higher rates) on AAR's financials, contributing modestly negative macro pressure on valuations.

cited_evidence.quote: The commercial aviation industry is historically cyclical and has been negatively affected in the past by geopolitical events, conflicts and wars, weather related events, natural disasters, pandemics, disruption to fuel and oil production and supply shortages, high fuel and oil prices, environmental concerns

macro.rationale: The evidence window references rising interest rates increasing net interest expense significantly year-over-year, and macro-level aviation industry risks including fuel costs, geopolitical uncertainty, and government budget constraints, all of which represent a modestly negative macro backdrop even as commercial travel recovered.

attribution.model_notes: live attribution: The -4.8% move (vol_zscore=-3.27, magnitude_rank=1.0) is notably severe relative to the evidence available. The most proximate negative catalyst is the August 28 8-K disclosing a ~$25M non-cash pre-tax pension settlement charge in Q1 FY2024, filed just 8 days before the move date. However, a non-cash charge of this size on a ~$2B revenue company should not alone justify a ~5% decline; the fundamental demand, pricing, and management execution signals from the July 18 earnings call were all constructive. The move is therefore classified as transient — likely an overreaction to the pension charge disclosure, possibly compounded by thin late-summer liquidity (vol_zscore suggests an unusually large move). Confidence is moderate at 0.42 because no specific news event on September 5 itself is documented in the evidence, and the magnitude of the move exceeds what the pension charge alone would warrant. cited_evidence.quote: Our second quarter sales of $686 million improved 26% from the same quarter last year. These results, which included double-digit sales growth for both our commercial and government businesses, clearly demonstrate the strong demand for our aftermarket aircraft and engine services.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Record quarterly sales with double-digit growth in both commercial and government segments directly signals robust demand, a primary positive driver for the stock move.

cited_evidence.quote: The strong demand is not slowing down, and we expect it to continue through calendar year 2025.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management's explicit forward demand guidance reinforces that demand strength is not transient, raising the dimension's positive weight.

cited_evidence.quote: Promotion sales increased 30% during the quarter compared to 20% from the prior quarter. This additional increase in sales growth came from all three of our core business segments, led by commercial distribution and product support sales.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Acceleration in commercial sales growth across all segments, from 20% to 30%, confirms broad-based demand improvement supporting the demand dimension.

demand.rationale: Record Q2 sales of $686M (+26% YoY) with organic growth accelerating to 12%, driven by strong commercial and government demand across all three segments, with explicit forward guidance that demand will continue through calendar 2025.

cited_evidence.quote: EBITDA margin increased from 10.1% in the second quarter of last year to 11.4% this year. Our adjusted operating margin increased from 8.1% last year to 9.2% this year.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Meaningful margin expansion signals that pricing power and mix improvements are positively contributing, supporting the pricing dimension as positive.

cited_evidence.quote: I'd say the pricing, the values are still very, very strong. I would not say that they are accelerating at the rates that we had seen, but they're still very, very strong. And, of course, the prices that we pay for assets we're able to command equally strong pricing in the market to preserve our spreads.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management's commentary on sustained strong USM pricing and preserved spreads indicates pricing dimension remains positive, though not accelerating.

pricing.rationale: Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded from 10.1% to 11.4% and operating margin from 8.1% to 9.2%, with management citing exclusive distribution contracts commanding higher margins and USM pricing remaining 'very, very strong' on legacy engines.

cited_evidence.quote: we continue to see engine-related growth opportunities, such as the recently announced exclusive distribution agreement with Chromalloy to sell their PMA parts for the CF6-80C2 engine type. Securing this agreement further expands our engine part offering and demonstrates the value we bring to both suppliers and customers.
cited_evidence.reasoning: New exclusive distribution agreements expand AAR's moat against competitors and indicate the company is winning contracts that peers do not have access to, positively affecting the competitive dimension.

cited_evidence.quote: I believe we're doing a good job of outperforming the competition and getting our hands on assets ahead of them. So I would not characterize this as a trend related to increased production out of the OEM.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management's explicit claim of outperforming competition in asset sourcing reinforces a positive competitive position narrative, supporting a positive direction for this dimension.

cited_evidence.quote: the exclusive contract focus for our distribution business allows us to earn higher margins than traditional distribution models due to the added value that we provide to both suppliers and customers.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The structural advantage of exclusive contracts versus traditional distribution models underscores AAR's durable competitive differentiation, a positive signal for the competitive dimension.

competitive.rationale: New exclusive distribution agreements (Chromalloy for CF6-80C2 PMA parts, Wipany/TransDigm), a joint venture with Air France for Asia-Pacific MRO, and management's emphasis on winning assets ahead of competitors all signal competitive position strengthening.

cited_evidence.quote: Regarding the balance of the fiscal year and for Q3 specifically, we expect year-over-year sales growth of 22% to 25% and adjusted operating margin in the range of 9.2% to 9.4%.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Specific and upward-sloping Q3 guidance issued alongside a strong beat reinforces management's credibility and forward visibility, a key positive driver for management_credibility.

cited_evidence.quote: on December 19, we announced that we have reached resolutions with the Department of Justice and the Securities Exchange Commission to resolve our previously disclosed potential violations of the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act. The total amount associated with the settlement is $55.6 million. This has been a long process. We are pleased that this issue is now settled.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Resolution of the multi-year FCPA overhang removes regulatory uncertainty that had weighed on investor confidence, meaningfully boosting management_credibility perception.

cited_evidence.quote: we entered into a definitive agreement to sell our landing gear overhaul business to GA Telesis for $51 million. This strategic decision was made so that we could put an even greater focus on our higher growth, higher margin aftermarket activities.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The strategic divestiture of a lower-margin business, executed at a time of record results, demonstrates focused portfolio management and capital discipline, positively signaling management credibility.

management_credibility.rationale: Management delivered on prior guidance with record Q2 results, raised Q3 guidance for 22-25% YoY sales growth and 9.2-9.4% operating margins, resolved the long-overhang FCPA matter, and announced the accretive divestiture of the lower-margin landing gear business — all enhancing credibility.

cited_evidence.quote: The overall macro environment for commercial aviation remains very strong. In addition, we have multiple levers to continue to drive significant growth.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management's explicit characterization of the macro environment as 'very strong' for commercial aviation supports a positive macro direction, though this is a broad tailwind already embedded in demand rather than a standalone new catalyst.

cited_evidence.quote: many different stocks in the stock market over the past month or so have been under pressure due to the rise in interest rates with the ten-year yield spiking, and we were wondering what is your long-term view of net leverage and your appetite for M&A in regards to the recent increase in interest rates

macro.rationale: Management cited elevated consumer air travel, an aging fleet, and rising interest rates as sector-wide forces; while rising rates create some risk, the overwhelming macro backdrop for commercial aviation aftermarket remains strongly favorable.

macro.rationale: Ongoing Middle East conflict (Strait of Hormuz closure, elevated oil prices) had created significant macro headwinds for aviation-exposed stocks in preceding sessions, but AAR's Q3 results and commentary partially offset this by demonstrating demand resilience; net macro remains a modest negative overhang.

attribution.model_notes: live attribution: The +9.9% move is well-explained by a confluence of company-specific positives: a broad earnings beat (EPS +3.3% vs. estimate, revenue +4.7% vs. estimate), a formal full-year guidance raise, four consecutive quarters of beats, ahead-of-schedule HAECO integration, and management's effective rebuttal of macro fears (airline demand remains strong despite Middle East conflict). The macro backdrop (elevated oil, Middle East war, broad market down) was a negative that was largely priced in via prior-week declines (-5.2% on 3/21), making the earnings release a catalyst for recovery and further re-rating. The move is classified as structural because the guidance raise and execution track record suggest lasting fundamental improvement rather than a one-time occurrence.
